column36: Comments

column2: Emperors, Queens, and One Very Confused Spider
column36:
First I want to say thanks to Kylah, as although Hydra Base are not villains, they do KO and that is pretty neat. Setup felt fair, although I'm sure my hero choices were suboptimal, however they did counter the Mastermind's Throne's Favor the most. Medusa and Black bolt are pretty 'meh' heroes, and in both games it showed.

I was succesful in keeping the escape pile empty, but in both games only one deck took off. In attempt 1 it was deck 2 with a strong Beta Ray Bill deck. In the second game it was Deck 1 with a strong Black Bolt focused deck. Attempt 1 led to an earlier win and 10 fewer points over 4 fewer turns, so I guessed that was the better choice to submit. Both games I did notice some (probably on puropse) anti-synergy between High-Tech Spider-Man and Hydra Base for Danger Sense. Only Villains increase attack and although they are Henchmen, they are Locations and not Villains. Was still nice to see what cards would come up though.

The Shi'ar villains were a bit annoying, and also a bit helpful. I can see people without extra acces to Throne's Favor struggle to counter the bad stuff from Strikes, MM effect and villain Ambushes. Black Bolt and Medusa aren't the greatest Heroes though, so it's a kind of trade-off. I never managed to defeat Emperor Vulcan two times in one turn. But in attempt 1, by some luck (I did get Medusa's rare) I could help hit Emperor Vulcan two separate times with the far weaker built deck.

Scheme Twists in this setup were not too bad to handle, easily kept under 5 'missed' ones. Master Strikes were actually annoying, especially in game 2 where the strong deck got 2 wounds from them amongst otherwise perfectly strong mostly 3 attack cards. There was enough KO to get one deck rid of (almost) all starters, but not enough for both decks. That's why only one deck did well both attempts.

A fun first challenge and I did plan my time well enough to complete my games before the deadline. Kylah, please keep giving me KO Henchmen and I'll be grateful.

What a blast!  Maybe it’s just week 1 talking, but I am so glad to be back in tournament season.  I knew going against Vulcan I had to control thrones favor and the only options for that were Medusa and Black Bolt, and they did not disappoint.  But the real hero was Crystal.  I was pulling off some stellar damage with her cost 3 card.  The chance to do 5 damage for just 3 points is awesome.  Also, great call with hydra base as the henchmen, it made some of the micro size changers tough, but also helped mitigate early escapes from the city filling before your damage popped up.  Very fun start.  Also, last time I’ll be in the pod at the top of the page.

Felt like a pretty average setup in terms of the set up, but player two had a great run. Built a deck with Photon and a single Stormbreaker. I could play out my whole deck at the end and I could produce exactly 13 attack to take out Vulcan even if he had the Thrones Favor.

Vulcan is really not one of my favorite MM and High Tech Spiderman was a pain for this setup. My first game went a bit faster, but I had some Escapes, so I submitted my clean but slower victory from the second attempt.
Deck one tried for Worthy cards and Crystal - never quite got to full efficiency but did enough to get by.  Deck two was Spiderman and Tech - again, not as efficient as I'd like but did the thing.

Definitely struggled to generate enough attack and pull off Crtsral's abilities. But Black Bolt helped stop Throne's Favor from being a problem and worked well with Beta Ray Bill. Danger Sense helped too! Not my strongest start but it was fun to try and figure this scenario out.

Fun game. I went with Jean Grey to take advantage of Hidden Witnesses, and Mockingbird for extra undercover shenanigans. I wasn't sure how the scheme would affect things, but it really swung the games.

My first game I got a few good Jean Grey cards by winning blackjack, and that hand ended up carrying the game. The second hand never got started. My second game the Mastermind got a 21 on 3 out of 4 blackjack games, then beat my 19 with a 20 on the 5th twist :(  The game wasn't going well before I lost either.

Extra cheese for all but one gamble attempt and proceeded to win every single one except their last. Pizza had one gamble attempt and lost. Pizza became money person to buy the hidden witnesses so that deathlok phil coulson Extra cheese could pummel everything away. 

“I love gambling” -Extra cheese
We tried to get Morbius going on with red cards and Wolverine, but it never really worked. Black deck did his thing and we got lucky on 2 blackjack hands.

Fun set up and I really enjoyed my heroes. Managed to make a nice Spidey / Elektra / Morbius deck that cleaned the board in game 2. First game ended suddenly with a twist right before I could finish it. Shield Level was a fun approach even though the set up did not help directly.

P2 had a good thin Covert deck with lots of Recruit. P1 had a Spider-Man Noir deck with very little, but managed a couple of hits when there were no HWs around. I was on 3 wagered souls for ~3 Twists before the end. Second game was over quickly after failing the first four Twists.
It really helped winning a turn one scheme twist to get a 6 cost cable to help keep generate the attack needed to hit MM almost every other turn

column36: I decided I didn't believe in KO for this setup and just focused on being able to meet the minimum recruit with draw to get attack to hit him
Captain Marvel and M were definitely the MVPs here. One side focused on strength, the other on ranged (especially since Ant-Man was the first hero purged from the timeline)!

We thought the assigned heroes were a bit of an odd choice as they didn't really mesh with the balance of setup either as a boost or a nerf. It felt like we could play with only 7 Scheme Twists and Agent X-13 and M already in the KO pile (would've made shuffling easier). We liked the strategy of managing who's next on the chopping block and Nimrod is always a fun Mastermind. 
Great setup to start the Tourney. Looking forward to the next few weeks.

Not too bad, we might've gotten lucky with Scheme Twists so we were able to keep a few key Heroes for our decks for the majority of the game. I went with Ant-Man (and potentially some big feet to Nimrod) while my partner went with Lady Thor at first but shifted to M's strategy of cards that cost 3.
Purged  sequence  strong guy for first, wonder man for second  and M .luchy game for villand deck, i found colossus   2 time in critical time😁

column32: I did not make a second attempt.
column33: I did not make a second attempt.
column36:
Almost did not have time to do this, was not even really in the mood for starting the tournament. But this turned out to be a fun start for the tourney and my enthusiasm has returned! 
I picked black knight for more possible empower as the hero pool gets smaller (did not end up having any of those common cards). I picked winter soldier mostly just because why not. (Not a strong hero generally but thought it will synergize well with ironman card draw). 
In the end I sacrificed M, X-13, Strong Guy quite early. Wonderman empower cards turned out to be my savior in the endgame, with the HQ getting down to only Jocasta and SIM, providing many blue empower. Probably many others will be faster than me, but could get most high VP villains, so it did not turned out bad in the end.

Awesome first round setup. Another fantastic example of how much I enjoy Legendary. I'd never played this scheme before so the dynamic of extra Heroes was super fun. A great game in my eye is one of two things...shifting into high gear and cruising to victory, or a tight game that comes down to the final turns. This was the latter. Loved the tension that was built as the hero selection dropped. I probably could've switched things up after the first game, but decided to enjoy the setup as I had it. Some fun synergies and battles made this a great dive back into Legendary after a number of weeks away from it. Let's go!!

Had a good time with this one. We had a hard time getting our decks where we wanted in Game 1, but Game 2 went better. I thought my friend was going to have an aneurysm when he pulled his 4th or 5th Twist in a row. Sometimes Legendary can be a cruel mistress.

Did not expect a timer scheme that added three heroes to be so difficult, but I probably deserved losing twice because I chose captain America 9141 over proxima midnight. I really like the fact that this scheme shows who your favorites are out of the twelve, but every time I had a huge attack hand with only 5 recruit really sucked.

column36: White Wolf and King Black Panther were very helpful to keep the thrones favor off of the mastermind. We managed to get both their ultimates as well. A big help for us was getting White Wolf's (Reflective Vibranium Armor) which managed to allow us to KO the masterstrike a few times this game and avoid the MM from getting another uru weapon. Combine that with the wounding potentials, and it helped us keep the nasty uru enchanted weapons motley crew at bay. It was unfortunate early on to have one escape but this was certainly a tough scenario!

Game 1 we got unlucky with some unavoidable escapes early on but managed to win faster. Game 2 we had one less escape with higher points per turn but did manage to take a bit longer to win. Both games were tough where we had built decent decks, but things never really seemed to line up just right where we would miss hitting the mastermind or a villain by 1 attack or having to discard a card from getting demolished. Altogether a fun setup but we have not watched game of thrones so we cannot appreciate the reference. Sorry!

At the begining of the game I got some strong villains entering the city which contributed to the escape of both Strange (Ouch) and Widow. Luckily I got a lot of Stingrays and Doctor Dooms in the HQ which allowed me to make one hand draw focused and the other one undercover focused. At the end I was able to ramp up a lot of damage and draw which helped me defeat the mastermind tactics quickly to then focus on the villains.

It's not all bad though with a majority of the Heroic Ambushes going off which really helped on getting the first hit on the Mastermind. Soon as I saw Infernal Chainsaw hit the HQ I knew I was in for a good game and at the very end a last minute Howard brought a big one two hit on the MM and City

Lots of high value VP villains made Uru Iron Man tough to hit early on. I had to wait till later when I knew scheme twists were in the villain deck in order to hit him . This was tough, but I won both of my efforts. I just picked the better of the two

column36: Very frustrating with all the unknown attacks needed due to the Uru-Enchanted weapons.  You get a good hand, 14 attack or so, and then the 7 MM becomes 19 attack and your turn is wasted.  Not a fan of that much uncertainty.
2S beat 3J for President by a landslide!

First game actually finishes 2 turns faster and had more points, but sadly had one escapes Still a decent game, went with Howard to go with the other Unaffiliated heroes and Squirrel Girl to be able to get more Spider-Men to send as president. Always fun to fight Jonah and at least he made the Symbiotic Armor that showed up in both games less annoying.

I really stressed over hero choice, but Spider-woman and GR 2099 both worked great.  I was able to send tons of cards undercover.  In game 2, I even got shield officers to help get more cards undercover.  At the end bell, Spidey was up 80,000 votes to JJJ's 60,000, and GR 2099 sat at 10,000 votes (b/c Spidey didn't show up early and I panicked).  Really fun run.  I had hardly used 2099 and loved it.  The hands even got complicated enough I used my attack and recruit counters to keep everything straight more than half the game.

JJJ thought he could run a smear campaign against Spider-Man to win his presidency, but unfortunately, he was no match for the web-slinger. It didn't help that he had decided to don his own symbiote armour in his efforts to crush crime, which only raised questions from his angry supporters and subsequently loss him the election.

I think it's that Loki 2099 Villain that puts your Master Strikes back in the Villain Deck?  Man I hit that twice early, ended up playing 5 Master Strikes (with 2 left in the Villain Deck).  That Angry Mob pile was pretty deep before I could finally start whittling it down.  Once I got it down, made pretty quick work of the Mastermind.
Haven't played Legendary in months! Do this was the first game in a long time...really enjoyed it...! Enjoyed both the Angry Mob and Votes to have to manage on top of city amd villains...

Just felt like an average game to be honest. JJJ was meh, but the hero combination was very fun. We got kinda unlucky in the first game drawing 2099 Thor back to back and gaining 2 wounds each off the bat in the first round, and in the second attempt we gambled and lost. Overall, decent first pod and play, excited to see what we will play in the future!
First game was very fun, heroes synergized well. Second time was a slog, got expensive cards in hq and couldn't buy em.
I don't like Man Thing. Every time I play with him I like him less. I will say Hulk 2099 was great with Howard the duck.

Very fun. Ego has such a clever and different design and pairing him with a scheme that floods the city was great. Both games saw periods where the extended city was near capacity and I was sweating trying to clear it out in time.

First game I got Piercing Energy working better than I ever have, which allowed me to clear the city, but the Gamora/Storm/Black Panther side took a little longer to get going. This resulted in a longer game, and ended with Ego causing a Patrol Craft to escape on his final Tactic >:(

Second game Piercing Energy took longer to get going and didn't work as well, but G/S/BP side got COOKING thanks to all 3 Retractable Swords, resulting in a shorter game and no cheap Ego escape.

Shoutout to The Orb for clearing out a HQ clogged w/ unwanted Hulkling cards at crucial moments in both games!

column36: Fantastic setup, and super creative! I also enjoyed the flavor of combining Brood Queen from WWH and The Brood from X-Men. This Scheme was very cool with Murderworld and the Sapien League. Getting to see the top card of the Villain Deck after fighting a Sapien League then let me strategically decide when to "scan" that card once it was in the HQ. I could get very tactical about when I flipped them up. For instance, knowing to play out my cards to have no cards "in hand" before I scanned a Master Strike and triggered FFF's Demolish (or just simply know to avoid the MS if that was safer). There was a very satisfying amount of deck-thinning and manipulation, setting up well-timed Feasts and Berserks. First time playing with a lot of these cards, and it was a fantastic way to try them! I ended up having a high turn count, so I doubt I'll be competitive this week, but I just enjoyed each turn and didn't want the fun to stop! Great start to the tourney!

First attempt was so lucky, was able to thin a deck through feasting, with Brainstorm stacking the top of the deck.  My wife unended up with most of the Twists-turned-wounds, which kinda pissed her off, but made my deck all the better.  Had Miek and Super-Skrull rares, and was able to hit FingFangFoom basically every turn. Second attempt proved how lucky the first was - it was a wreck and we ended up going through the villian deck while hitting mastermind only once.

It was tight one, however a solid mix of classes for player 1 enabled a couple of double hits on Fin Fang Foom before getting the final blow in with 4 cards left in the deck. Fntastic Four to the rescue!

column36: If I had more time I would play this one again. I really enjoyed mixing Mystique, speed and Ronin together. I was able to use multiple man to get rid of most of my starting cards for player 1 which let me have a small and power deck. The combo of Speed's Race to the Rescue with Hyperspeed and dodge cards allowed me to draw a bunch of strong cards to clear the city of villains quickly. My second player hand wasnt great. I tried to build a star lord and dark hawk deck and really dark hawk was the best part of it but I couldnt get rid of my 0 cost cards so my deck was flooded with poor cards. Anyway - great game!

Brutal. I only survived due to lucky shuffles every time Elite Assassins went back into the deck in the second half of the game. Lot of early escapes and a super full city. My tech deck for P2 did not take off like I hoped. Not a huge fan of Last Stand or Dark Memories, but I think my instinct to avoid multiple hero colors in order to keep DM low when DM heroes were available was the wrong choice. Leaning into heroes that make your Dark Memories even stronger would have probably ended up better.
Almost ten years playing Legendary.  Well over 400 plays.  That was by far the most diabolical setup I have ever encountered.

This setup was EXTREMELY rough. Unfortunately we forgot to record our first game, which really wasn't much better but we at least won, so we were forced to submit the second game, which was VERY bad! The wounds were too overwhelming and were practically impossible to deal with. We drowned almost immediately. I think the setup was too aggressive and needed a way to deal with the wounds (i.e. instead of Multiple Man, use a henchmen that can KO wounds).

column36: Great game! Iceman and bishop are just so good and with Fantomex I was able to get a lot of nice x sequences and trigger KO from bishops common. Each side had only 3 starters left in their deck by the end!

Iron Fist was a great complement to Fantomex. Elektra wasn't as helpful as anticipated. This was our first time playing against Omega Red and our concerns about the inability to efficiently KO Wounds ended up not being as bad as we though. We lost our first one due to clumped, early STs but we were on the path to victory otherwise with that attempt.
Used Blade to keep moving Villains out of the Blood Bank so I didn't have to worry about the scheme twists as much. Colossus helped block some wounds. Great setup - Omega Red is a great MasterMind

column36: I'm low on time this week so I was prob rushing a bit too much. I love Fantomex. He's so fun. P1 scaled off Fantomex, and got a bit unlucky at first but turned it around. And Blade's rare is kinda nuts, w/ two sets of henchmen (even w/ the blood frenzy). The setup and playing around the blood bank felt really fun, especially w/ things like haunts and Blade to manipulate the city with. Barely won, I dealt the final blow on the last possible turn. Fun game

column36:
I really wanted to try and make Skaar work. Knowing Omega Red makes wounds difficult I thought he would come in handy. But even in this setup, I didn't manage to create that much damage with him. Weapon H (his intended partner) carried more for player one. 
Player 2 carried the game, though. Gaining three rares, including Jessica Jones' which allowed payer two to bury twists, was crucial. Stars of the players were Fantomex and Weapon H!

For me it was the first time using in full game capacity Weapon H, Skaar, and Jessica Jones (Heroes) , Circus of Crime (Henchmen), Sire Vampires (Scheme), and Omega Red (MM).

Thanks!
An early BFG and solid Weapon X sequence definitely helped.

First game went smoothly with amadeus complimenting Moon Knight and she-hulk and Joe fixit getting a recruit engine going. Some early twists made the early game slow but once things got going I was able to wrap up the mastermind quickly.

Second game was looking good with early rares from Joe and she-hulk but one hand had to take some early gamora which just pumped up villains in the city for little benefit. Still a win but took 7 extra turns to do it.
Really fun MM and scheme combination. Allows you to choose multiple strategies, that made it more challenging but fun to play.

Game was fun, first round decks got to be played how he wanted with a nice Guardian of the Galaxy synergy with She-Hulk and Joe Fixit together. 

Game 2 had a bad scheme twist break but it happens and She-Hulk didn't want to come and play.

Clone Saga is not my favorite scheme, and discarding down to three cards nearly all the time (despite trying to recruit plenty of repeats) dragged out this game. Leaned into WWH heroes for my picks because I was excited to have this set for the tournament, but maybe I should have leaned into some more shard heavy heroes.

column36: Thanks!  Had fun.
S.H.I.E.L.D is one of my favorite sets to use and I always think they make for a great game.
Team 2 got a bystander engine quickly with help from Angel's card drawing mechanics and overrun the Mastermind with two huge rounds. Team 1 just got Spiderman and Elektra cards out of the way.
Due to an early pickup of Jean Greys uncommon, and some Black Widow, hand 1 drew their deck practically every turn!! Hit Mister Sinister 4 times in a single turn!!

Always love a Spider-Man game, always love a Widow game. In G2 I got all 3 of Spider-Man's uncommons which generated a ton of bystanders but very little attack power until BW's trump card came through. I kind of regret bringing Bishop; I didn't use any of his KO power and he failed to boost anything. If I'd had more time I probably would have chosen a different hero (maybe Storm?) for game 2, but I guess this worked out fine.
We just had villains escape too early, but we control the game very easily after we had some synchrony going. Fun round.

column36: I had some difficulties at first with the higher value hero cards entering the city as skulls, leading to a worrisome full city and an escape. However, the hero synergies started popping off and let me whittle down the city then keep it mostly clear. Sinister got powerful but the hero synergies were too much for him and her was defeated before too long. Definitely a fun game, especially the second half, but all the cars draws and rescues could be hard to track so maybe not the best game for newbies.

This was a tough one to pick which round to go with, we won both, but the first one had way more bystanders rescued, but a lot more escaped villains/heroes, the second one was faster and less escapes, but less bystanders rescued. We ultimately went with the second one.

Spiderman ended up being a bit of a black sheep for us, but my wife got him off the ground late in round one alongside black widow. My winning combo the first round was Invisible Woman and Mr. Fantastic. The second round my wife had trouble getting anywhere with Black Widow and some scattered Spiderman again. I went mostly Mr. Fantastic and Iron man, but also managed to grab BW's rare which really sealed it.
